data.title: Reconciliation / Trade Break Analyst
data.description: Investigate and resolve position, cash, and trade breaks between internal systems, custodians, and counterparties. Daily recon cycles, escalation, root-cause fixes.
data.why: Direct match to your Bloomberg recon work (20–25 completed weekly, peak 33). This is your highest-probability lane — search this title first.

data.title: SMA / Managed Accounts Operations Analyst
data.description: Account onboarding, restrictions, recon files, and PM/sponsor support for separately managed account and UMA platforms.
data.why: You supported 650+ SMAs and CITs at Bloomberg — you can claim this experience by name. Best pay-to-fit ratio of any lane.

data.title: Middle Office / Trade Support Analyst
data.description: Trade capture, confirmation, allocation, and lifecycle management between the desk and back office. Fix fails and mismatches before settlement.
data.why: Natural next step from recon; large seat count at every bank in NYC and Jersey City.

data.title: Client Service / PM-Facing Operations Analyst
data.description: Operational point of contact for portfolio managers, sponsors, or institutional clients — inquiries, onboarding, reporting, issue resolution.
data.why: You were the point of contact for 400+ institutional PMs — the exact story these roles hire for.

data.title: Corporate Actions Analyst
data.description: Process mandatory and voluntary corporate actions (dividends, splits, tenders), reconcile entitlements, notify holders.
data.why: Recon-adjacent, detail-driven, and often paired with reconciliation in the same posting (e.g. Oppenheimer).

data.title: Fund / Investor Services Analyst (incl. Alternatives)
data.description: Investor onboarding, capital calls/distributions, transfers, and reporting for mutual funds, CITs, or private funds.
data.why: Your CIT experience maps here; alternatives ops (Lord Abbett-style seats) is a growing area with early-career openings.

data.title: Asset Servicing / Custody Operations Analyst
data.description: Safekeeping, income processing, tax reclaims, and client asset servicing at custody banks.
data.why: Custody banks (BNY, State Street, Northern Trust) hire this profile in volume — the numbers game works in your favor here.

data.title: Settlements Analyst
data.description: Ensure trades settle on time across markets; manage fails, buy-ins, and counterparty communication.
data.why: New product knowledge but the same break-resolution muscle; Goldman-tier settlements seats pay near the top of your range.

data.title: Product Control / P&L Analyst
data.description: Daily P&L production and validation for a trading desk; explain P&L drivers, reconcile front-to-back.
data.why: Higher pay and a real trajectory (desk-facing finance). Your MBA + recon background gets you in the interview; prep the accounting story.

data.title: Valuations / Pricing Analyst (IPV)
data.description: Independent price verification of positions; source vendor prices, investigate stale/broken marks.
data.why: Bloomberg market-data fluency is a genuine edge; avoid the deeply quant versions (non-linear rates etc.).

data.title: Market / Counterparty Risk Analyst
data.description: Monitor exposures, margin, and limits; produce risk reporting for trading businesses.
data.why: Pays well and values process discipline; skip postings that require heavy SQL/Python.

data.title: Operations Transformation / Process Automation Analyst
data.description: Improve and automate operations workflows — low-code tools, AI adoption, process redesign inside ops teams.
data.why: Your stated preference — the AI/automation angle. Fewer postings, but your MBA analytics concentration + ops background is the intended profile.
